coarse_alignment_reason: Whole-poem fallback because Chinese and English stanza structures do not align safely.
chinese_text:
子之丰兮，俟我乎巷兮。悔予不送兮。
子之昌兮，俟我乎堂兮。悔予不將兮。
衣錦褧衣，裳錦褧裳。叔兮伯兮，駕予與行。
裳錦褧裳，衣錦褧衣。叔兮伯兮，駕予與歸。
translation_text:
1 Full and good looking was the gentleman,
Who waited for me in the lane !
I repent that I did not go with him.

2 A splendid gentleman was he,
Who waited for me in the hall !
I regret that I did not accompany him.

3 Over my embroidered upper robe, I have put on a [plain]
single garment ;
Over my embroidered lower robe, I have done the same.
O Sir, O Sir,
Have your carriage ready for me to go with you.

4 Over my embroidered lower robe, I have put on a [plain]
single garment ;
Over my embroidered upper robe, I have done the same.
O Sir, 0 Sir,
Have your carriage ready to take me home with you.
